popularStory.description: Alfonso VI (c.?1040/1041-1 July 1109), nicknamed the Brave (El Bravo) or the Valiant, was the son of King Ferdinand I of León and Queen Sancha, daughter of Alfonso V and sister of Bermudo III. He beca …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon於1040年6月15日出生在西班牙加利西亞武倫瑟洛比斯可波斯特拉時，他的父親Fernando el Magno I Rey de Castilla y XIV de León22歲，母親Reina Sancha I de León27歲。他於1069年與Constance de Bourgogne在法國圖勞斯結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1109年7月在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多享年69歲，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安利安薩哈奧。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon于1040年6月15日出生在西班牙加利西亞武倫瑟洛比斯可波斯特拉时，他的父亲Fernando el Magno I Rey de Castilla y XIV de León22岁，母亲Reina Sancha I de León27岁。他于1069年与Constance de Bourgogne在法国圖勞斯结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。他去世于1109年7月在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多享年69岁，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安利安薩哈奧。

nameMeanings.name: Rey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Luis, Juan, Alfredo, Roberto, Alfonso, Angel, Eduardo, Jesus, Mariano, Carlos.

Spanish, Galician, and French (Occitan): from Spanish and Old French rey ‘king’ (from Latin rex, genitive regis), which may have denoted someone in the service of a king, or it may have been from the title of someone in a brotherhood or a nickname for someone who behaved in a regal fashion or who had earned the title in some contest of skill or by presiding over festivities. Compare Del Rey and Galician Rei .
English (Somerset): variant of   Ray  .

variants.name: Del Rey
meanings: Spanish: variant of Rey , with the preposition and definite article del ‘from/of the’.

variants.name: Reyes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Carlos, Manuel, Luis, Jesus, Pedro, Ruben, Miguel, Roberto, Francisco, Ramon.
Spanish:
plural form of   Rey  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Castilla
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alfredo, Carlos, Raul, Sergio, Jesus, Jorge, Pablo, Ruben, Blanca, Elido, Francisco, Guillermo.
Spanish: habitational name from Castile (Spanish Castilla), a region in central Spain. The name derives from the many castles in the region. This surname is also found in southern France.
History: As an independent kingdom between the 10th and 15th centuries, Castile formed the largest power in the Iberian peninsula.

variants.name: Castiglia
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Annamarie, Antonino, Benedetto, Carmine, Francesco, Gaetano, Gennaro, Giacomo, Giuseppe, Ignazio.
Italian: habitational name from Castile (Italian Castiglia) in central Spain (see Castilla ).

nameMeanings.name: Leon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Pedro, Jesus, Jorge, Francisco, Rafael, Miguel, Raul.

Spanish (León): habitational name from León, a city in northwestern Spain, named with Latin legio, genitive legionis ‘legion’, a division of the Roman army. In Roman times the city was the garrison of the 7th Legion, known as the Legio Gemina. The city's name became shortened from Legion(em) to Leon(em), and in this form developed an unetymological association with the word for ‘lion’ (Spanish león).

French (Léon) and Spanish (León): from the personal name Léon, León, from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn. The name Leo(n) was borne by numerous early Christian martyrs and thirteen popes.

variants.name: Len
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Zbigniew, Jozef, Karol.
Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a flax grower or merchant, from Polish len ‘flax’.
Polish (Leń): nickname from leń ‘lazybones’.

variants.name: Leonides
meanings: Hispanic (Leónides; Mexico): from the personal name Leónides, from Greek Leonidēs, itself most likely a patronymic derivative of Leōn (from leōn ‘lion’; see Leon ) + the suffix -(e)ides.

variants.name: Lyon
meanings: Scottish, English, and French: from the personal name Middle English Lyon, Old French Leon (from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn; see Leon ). Compare Lyall .
Scottish and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lyons-la-Forêt in Eure, Normandy. It is unlikely to be from the better-known southern French city of Lyon (see 5 below).

English and French: nickname from Middle English lioun ‘lion’ (Old English, Latin leo), Old French leon, perhaps applied to a brave, fierce, or proud person, or one with a shaggy mane of hair. Compare Lion .

variants.name: Leos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Pedro, Francisco, Manuel, Ruben, Carlos, Juan, Santos, Jesus, Pablo, Raul, Alfonso.
Hispanic (mainly Mexico): altered form of Basque Leoz, itself a Castilianized form (also Léoz) of a habitational name from Leotz (in Castilian: Leoz) in Navarre.
Greek: from the personal name Leos, a pet form of Leon .

variants.name: Liang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Jian, Feng, Wen, Wei, Ping, Ying, Cheng, Guang, Jin, Jing, Liang, Chang, Chung, Hu, Nam, Pang, Jang, Min, Neng, Shen, Shiu, Sieu, Yeong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 梁, meaning ‘roofbeam’ or ‘bridge’ in Chinese: (i) from Liang (梁), the name of a state (located in present-day Hancheng in Shaanxi Province) which was granted to Kang, the youngest son of Qinzhong (died 822  BC ), because the latter made great contributions to the fight against minority ethnic groups in western China during the reign of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After the state was annihilated by the state of Qin in 641  BC , some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from the second element of the placenames Jie Liang (解梁), Gao Liang (高粱), and Qu Liang (曲梁), names of several cities originally within the border of the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province). In 645  BC , the Duke Hui of Jin (ruler of the state of Jin, died 637  BC ) ceded these cities to the state of Qin (located originally in present-day western Shaanxi province). Later, people from these cities adopted 粱 as their surname. (iii) from Liang (梁), also known as South Liang, the name of another state (located in present-day Longxi in Gansu Province) which was granted to Tang, son of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After this state was annihilated by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from the Ba Lie Lan (拔列蘭) family, members of an ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surname to 梁 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

variants.name: Leonti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Salvatore.
Italian (Sicily): from the Greek personal name or nickname Leon, Leontos ‘lion’ (equivalent of Latin Leo, Leonis; see Leon 2).

variants.name: Lew
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wing, Yuen, Fook, Jung, Ying, Young, Yung, Bin, Foon, Hong, Kam, Koon.
Polish: from the personal name Lew ‘lion’, adopted as a translation of Leon .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Lev  .

nameMeanings.name: Alfonso
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Visigothic: probably a compound of adal ‘noble’ + funs ‘ready’; a traditional royal name.

variants.name: Alonso
meanings: Variant of   Alfonso .

nameMeanings.name: VI
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

nameMeanings.name: Galicia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Modern Latinate form of   Alice .  It was borne by the British ballerina  Alicia Markova  (born  Lilian Alicia Marks ,  1910–2004 ).

variants.name: Alisha
meanings: Modern Latinate form of   Alice .  It was borne by the British ballerina  Alicia Markova  (born  Lilian Alicia Marks ,  1910–2004 ).

variants.name: Alisia
meanings: Modern Latinate form of   Alice .  It was borne by the British ballerina  Alicia Markova  (born  Lilian Alicia Marks ,  1910–2004 ).

variants.name: Alyssa
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alissa .

variants.name: Alissa
meanings: Contracted form of   Alicia .

variants.name: Alesha
meanings: Respelling of   Alicia .

en.lifeSummary: When Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon was born on 15 June 1040, in Compostela, Lobios, Ourense, Galicia, Spain, his father, Fernando el Magno I Rey de Castilla y XIV de León, was 22 and his mother, Reina Sancha I de León, was 27. He married Constance de Bourgogne in 1069, in Toulouse, France. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died in July 1109, in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain, at the age of 69, and was buried in Sahagún, León, Castilla y León, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon est né le 15 juin 1040, à Compostela, Lobios, Ourense, Galicia, Espagne, son père, Fernando el Magno I Rey de Castilla y XIV de León, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Reina Sancha I de León, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Constance de Bourgogne en 1069, à Toulouse, France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Il est décédé en juillet 1109, à Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterré à Sahagún, León, Castilla y León, Espagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon am 15. Juni 1040 in Compostela, Lobios, Ourense, Galicia, Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Fernando el Magno I Rey de Castilla y XIV de León 22 und seine Mutter Reina Sancha I de León 27. Er hat 1069 in Toulouse, Frankreich Constance de Bourgogne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb im Juli 1109 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien und wurde in Sahagún, León, Castilla y León, Spanien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon è nato il il 15 giugno 1040 nel Compostela, Lobios, Ourense, Galicia, Spagna, il suo padre, Fernando el Magno I Rey de Castilla y XIV de León, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Reina Sancha I de León, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Constance de Bourgogne nel 1069, nel Toulouse, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì nel luglio 1109, nel Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spagna, all'età di 69 anni, e fu sepolto a Sahagún, León, Castilla y León, Spagna.

ja.lifeSummary: Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leonが1040年6月15日にスペインガリシアオウレンゼロビオスコンプオステラで生まれた時，父Fernando el Magno I Rey de Castilla y XIV de Leónは22歳，母Reina Sancha I de Leónは27歳であった。1069年にフランストウロウゼでConstance de Bourgogneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼は1109年7月にスペインカスティラ-ラ マンチャトーレドで69歳で死去し，スペインカスティラ イ レoンレoンサハグuンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon(이)가 1040년 6월 15일 스페인 갈리샤 우렌스 로비오스 콤포스텔아에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Fernando el Magno I Rey de Castilla y XIV de León(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Reina Sancha I de León(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1069년 프랑스 토우루스에서 Constance de Bourgogne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1109년 7월 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 톨도에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 스페인 카스틸라 와이 레o 레o 사하그u에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon nasceu em 15 de junho de 1040, em Compostela, Lobios, Ourense, Galicia, Espanha, seu pai, Fernando el Magno I Rey de Castilla y XIV de León, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Reina Sancha I de León, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Constance de Bourgogne em 1069, em Toulouse, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em julho de 1109, em Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha, com 69 anos, e foi sepultado em Sahagún, León, Castilla y León, Espanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon родился 15 июня 1040 г. в местности компостела, лобиос, оуренс, галися, Испания, его отцу по имени Fernando el Magno I Rey de Castilla y XIV de León было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Reina Sancha I de León, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Constance de Bourgogne в 1069 г. в местности тоулоус, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Он умер июль 1109 г. в местности толедо, кастилла-ла манча, Испания в возрасте 69 лет и был похоронен в местности сахагuн, леoн, кастилла и леoн, Испания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon nació el 15 de junio de 1040, en Compostela, Lovios, Orense, Galicia, España, su papá, Fernando el Magno I Rey de Castilla y XIV de León, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Reina Sancha I de León, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Constance de Bourgogne en 1069, en Toulouse, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió en julio de 1109, en Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España, a la edad de 69 años, y fue sepultado en Sahagún, León, Castilla y León, España.
